repo_image: bre-repo:v1 (python:3.11-slim + git + COPY . /app + pip install pytest pymongo python-dateutil)
partial_note: Applied the oracle fix for only 2 of the 5 defects (the two zype_functions boundaries); the other 3 f2p tests still FAILED, so required-passed was 14/17 and reward was 0.
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN/vacuous: the repo ships no runnable test suite, so there is no visible red for the agent to chase; the 12 p2p adjacent-behavior tests pass at base+defects (verified in-image).
litmus_result: A strong dev given only instruction.md cannot land on a specific line in <2 min: the instruction names two engine directories and gives two representative symptoms without naming functions, files-of-plant, boundary directions, trigger values, or a count.
env_note: No task-specific env required. Targets are pure/deterministic/offline. pymongo and python-dateutil are installed only so the config/custom-function packages import; neither connects at import time. The IRE rule-evaluation module is loaded from its file path in the gold test to avoid importing its package __init__ (which pulls google-cloud-aiplatform / pymongo / openai).

defects.edge_input: a value exactly equal to the lower bound, e.g. evaluate(25000, 'between', [25000, 100000])
defects.wrong_output: returns False (reported outside the range) instead of True
defects.derivability: MEDIUM. Correct side (inclusive lower) is uniquely derivable from the in-repo parallel implementation of 'between' in custom_functions/base._apply_operator, which uses float(min) <= a <= float(max) on both ends, and from the fact that the planted line leaves the UPPER bound inclusive while making only the lower exclusive (asymmetry with no justification). No convention guess: a real second implementation pins it.
defects.why_no_ordinary_input_catches: Ordinary range checks feed values strictly inside [lo, hi]; only a == lo distinguishes < from <=. The 3 p2p between-tests (inside, on-upper-bound, below) agree under both variants.

defects.edge_input: a dot path selecting the first list element, e.g. get_value({'items':[{'name':'alpha'}]}, 'items.0.name')
defects.wrong_output: returns the default (None) as if the element were missing, instead of 'alpha'
defects.derivability: MEDIUM (probe-exposure noted). Correct side (index 0 valid) is pinned by the inline comment one line above: "Support array indexing: 'items.0.name'", which documents index-0 access as a supported feature; rejecting index 0 contradicts the documented behavior. Mild probe-exposure: an agent that specifically probes array indexing might try '.0.' first; the instruction does not signpost field extraction, so localization still requires reasoning.
defects.why_no_ordinary_input_catches: Object/dict dot paths and positive indices (>=1) are unaffected; only index exactly 0 changes. p2p covers index 1, nested dict, and out-of-range default.

defects.edge_input: an active PL/CC with sanctioned amount exactly on the ceiling, e.g. sanctionedAmountOrHighestCredit == '200000' with default max_limit 200000
defects.wrong_output: the account is dropped (has_qualifying_account False -> passed False) instead of qualifying (passed True)
defects.derivability: EASY. Correct side (inclusive ceiling) is pinned by the inline comment "Check if amount is within limit (up to max_limit)" and the class docstring "sanctioned amount up to specified limit (default 2 lakhs)". "up to X" is inclusive of X.
defects.why_no_ordinary_input_catches: Ordinary amounts are strictly below the ceiling (both variants qualify) or above it (neither qualifies); only amount == max_limit flips. p2p covers well-within and above-limit.

defects.edge_input: an account whose overdue is exactly the cutoff, e.g. amountOverdue == '3000' with default threshold 3000
defects.wrong_output: the account is flagged as high-overdue (has_high_overdue True -> passed False) instead of being excluded (passed True)
defects.derivability: EASY. Correct side (strict >) is pinned verbatim by the class docstring: "Exclude overdue up to ₹3000 from DPD computations. This checks if any account has overdue > threshold (default 3000)" — the cutoff value itself is excluded, and the docstring literally states the comparison as strict '>'.
defects.why_no_ordinary_input_catches: Overdue clearly above 3000 flags under both variants; clearly below 3000 passes under both; only overdue == 3000 flips. p2p covers above and below.

defects.edge_input: a score exactly at the configured minimum, e.g. score 700 with rule threshold 700
defects.wrong_output: status downgraded from PASS to WARNING ("below threshold but close") for a score that meets the minimum
defects.derivability: MEDIUM. Correct side (inclusive) is pinned by the PASS branch's own message string, f"Credit score {credit_score} meets minimum requirement of {threshold}": a score equal to the threshold MEETS the minimum requirement, which is exactly the PASS semantics; the FAIL branch message says "below minimum requirement". Deriving it requires reading the branch messages, not just the comparison.
defects.why_no_ordinary_input_catches: Scores comfortably above threshold PASS and scores below threshold-50 FAIL under both variants; only score == threshold flips PASS->WARNING. p2p covers above-threshold PASS and far-below FAIL.

derivability_summary: 2 EASY (docstring/inline-comment pinned: zype ActiveAccounts 'up to max_limit', zype AmountOverdue 'overdue > threshold') + 3 MEDIUM (require tracing to in-repo evidence: operator_handler 'between' vs the parallel base._apply_operator impl; field_extractor index-0 vs the 'items.0.name' example; credit-score band vs the 'meets minimum requirement' branch message). Zero convention-only picks: every correct side is fixed by concrete in-repo evidence, not by a taste call on which way a bare band-edge should go.
fairness_audit: Gold tests are pure-input unittest with ZERO mocks. Alternative-implementation audit: each f2p asserts an observable verdict/return value on an exact-edge input, not an oracle-chosen code path — any boundary correction that makes 'between' inclusive on the lower end, restores index-0 addressing, makes the ceiling/cutoff inclusive/strict per the docs, and makes the score band inclusive will pass. The IRE module is exec-loaded from its file so the test needs no cloud/DB SDK, and the instance is built with __new__ so no rule-file loader runs. No hidden test references a name that is absent at base. All five defected functions are live on the engine's rule-execution / recommendation-evaluation paths.
resistance_assessment: Targeting Opus 1-4/10. A solve requires finding and correcting ALL FIVE independent single-token boundary slips spread across 4 files and 2 top-level packages, with no failing test to chase (the repo has no runnable suite, removing the run-pytest-see-red gradient) and no visible git history of the plant. The two EASY defects are discoverable by careful reading of docstrings/comments; the three MEDIUM defects require tracing to a parallel implementation, an inline example, or a branch message. Breadth (5 tests over 4 source files in 2 packages), distance (a wrong operator/extraction helper surfaces only as a wrong eligibility verdict downstream), and the all-five-required gate together suppress the solve rate; an agent that only fixes the two literal examples in the instruction still fails 3 f2p.
